item.title: U.S. finishes 7th straight night of Iran strikes as Strait of Hormuz freezes up
item.description: In addition to the two U.S. service members killed, another is currently missing in action, U.S. Central Command said on Saturday.

item.title: Maps show wildfire smoke forecast, air quality alerts in swath of U.S.
item.description: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is engulfing large swaths of the Midwest and the U.S. East Coast this week.

item.title: 1 dead, 3 missing after boat sinks near Alcatraz Island in San Francisco Bay
item.description: One person died and three were missing in San Francisco Bay on Tuesday after a boat with 20 people on board sank near Alcatraz Island, authorities said.

item.title: Ukrainian drone attacks on Russia kill at least 9, injure over 60
item.description: Kyiv's forces are continuing their relentless aerial campaign​ against energy infrastructure and military targets inside Russia.

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Midland performs "Shooting Memories With Tequila"
item.description: Grammy-nominated trio Midland hail from the Texas country scene. Their new album "Stages" has been praised as one of the best traditional country albums of the year. Here's Midland with "Shooting Memories With Tequila."

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Midland performs "Drinkin' Problem"
item.description: Grammy-nominated trio Midland hail from the Texas country scene. Their new album "Stages" has been praised as one of the best traditional country albums of the year. Here's Midland with "Drinkin' Problem."

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Midland performs "Vaquero"
item.description: Grammy-nominated trio Midland hail from the Texas country scene. Their new album "Stages" has been praised as one of the best traditional country albums of the year. Here's Midland with "Vaquero."

item.title: Severe weather puts millions at risk in Northeast as wildfire smoke lingers
item.description: A severe weather threat is in effect from the Ohio Valley through the Mid-Atlantic and Northeast.

item.title: Roger Bennett on the impact of the World Cup, Messi's legacy
item.description: Roger Bennett, founder and CEO of the Men in Blazers Media Network, joins "CBS Saturday Mornings" to discuss the World Cup, Lionel Messi, and his memoir, "We Are the World (Cup)."

item.title: ICE detention center employee arrested for shooting protester in Colorado
item.description: A woman was shot while protesting outside of an ICE detention center in Aurora, Colorado, police say. The employee of a group operating the center faces assault and attempted second-degree murder charges in connection with the shooting. This comes after two fatal shootings by ICE agents in Maine and Texas.

item.title: Matt Damon and Anne Hathaway breathe new life into an ancient epic with "The Odyssey"
item.description: "The Odyssey" co-stars Matt Damon and Anne Hathaway share their experiences working with Oscar-winning director Christopher Nolan to interpret Homer's ancient poem.

item.title: Inside the winery that put Napa Valley on the map
item.description: The Robert Mondavi Winery opened in 1966, the first major winery in the Napa Valley since Prohibition. Now, the historic winery is experiencing a rebirth after a recent renovation.

item.title: Students earn scholarships, gain confidence when they "Do the Right Thing"
item.description: Nonprofit group Do the Right Thing honors young students who get good grades or help their communities, encouraging them to reach for their dreams. Organizers, educators and police talk about the organization's impact.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: Fans elated for Argentina and Spain to face off in World Cup final
item.description: Millions of fans will watch as Argentina and Spain appear in the World Cup final. Spain looks to dethrone reigning champion Argentina, while Argentina hopes Lionel Messi leads them to glory again.

item.title: License of Utah school where Paris Hilton alleged she was abused is revoked
item.description: The license for another campus of the Provo Canyon School was revoked on Friday.

item.title: White House doubles down on Trump's baseless election interference claims
item.description: President Trump rehashed old talking points about election security, claiming without evidence that China interfered in the 2020 election during a primetime address. A day later, Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin announced an election security crackdown.

item.title: Taylor Farms recalls iceberg lettuce after link to diarrhea-causing parasite
item.description: Lettuce from supplier Taylor Farms was linked to a parasitic disease in an FDA investigation. The FDA advised consumers should avoid shredded iceberg lettuce distributed to Taco Bell locations in Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, Ohio and West Virginia. Taco Bell said it removed the lettuce, and Taylor Farms voluntarily recalled their impacted product.

item.title: Canadian wildfire smoke blankets parts of U.S. as Texas floods turn deadly
item.description: Smoke from Canadian wildfires still shrouds huge swathes of the U.S., from the Great Lakes region to the Mid-Atlantic. Down south, torrential rain brought flooding to Texas Hill Country. Plus, a look at the air quality forecast.

item.title: 7/18: CBS Saturday Morning
item.description: Smoky air in parts of the U.S. as President Trump renews election interference claims. Plus, an interview with Anne Hathaway and Matt Damon.

item.title: What to know about Trump's claim that over 250K noncitizens are registered to vote
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin sent letters to four states alleging that a combined 250,000 noncitizens were registered to vote. Elections experts caution that could be a significant overcount.

item.title: 2 kidnapped Forest Service workers safe after being zip-tied, held at gunpoint
item.description: Two suspects, a father and his adult son who were allegedly armed with guns and knives, have been arrested, authorities said.

item.title: Model diagnosed with aggressive cancer after finding lump while shaving
item.description: Alison McCullough was in the best shape of her life and preparing for New York Fashion Week when she found a lump under her arm.

item.title: The Uplift: Weaving American history
item.description: We travel to South Boston, Virginia, where they are weaving pieces of American history every day at a patriotic flag factory. Plus, David Begnaud shares three heartwarming stories sent to him by viewers.

item.title: DOJ says it's no longer illegal to download TikTok on federal devices
item.description: The Justice Department determined this week a federal law banning TikTok from government devices no longer applies to the social video app.

item.title: Paul Pelosi faces misdemeanor hit-and-run charge for California collision
item.description: Paul Pelosi, the husband of former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi, has been charged with a misdemeanor hit-and-run count after authorities allege he struck a parked car and drove away from the scene.

item.title: Can you spot an AI image? Quiz shows how difficult identifying deepfakes has become.
item.description: An AI expert said the telltale signs people once relied on, such as distorted fingers, unnatural eyes, or other obvious visual flaws, have largely disappeared as technology has improved.

item.title: Taylor Farms pulls iceberg lettuce linked to cyclosporiasis outbreak
item.description: Taylor Farms said none of its branded salads or kits contain the iceberg lettuce associated with the outbreak.

item.title: Colorado ICE center employee arrested after allegedly shooting protester
item.description: An employee of a group that operates an ICE detainment center in Aurora, Colorado, was arrested after police said he shot and wounded a woman who was taking part in a protest.

item.title: "Master yachtsman" fugitive captured on boat after 21 years on the run
item.description: Ronald L. Fischer, who was featured on "America's Most Wanted," fled Rhode Island during his criminal trial for first-degree sexual assault in 2005.
